item.title: VitaBench 2.0: Evaluating Personalized and Proactive Agents in Long-Term User Interactions
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) have evolved into interactive agents that collaborate with users in real-world tasks. Effective collaboration in such settings increasingly depends on understanding the user beyond what is explicitly stated,...

item.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
item.description: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has become the de facto architecture for hundred-billion-parameter language models, yet its advantages at sub-billion scales for on-device deployment remain largely unexplored. To close this gap, we present Mobil...

item.title: Learning to Act under Noise: Enhancing Agent Robustness via Noisy Environments
item.description: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have facilitated the widespread deployment of LLMs as interactive agents capable of reasoning, planning, and tool use. Despite strong performance on existing benchmarks, such agents often e...

item.title: FastKernels: Benchmarking GPU Kernel Generation in Production
item.description: LLM-based agents for GPU kernel generation are advancing rapidly, yet their progress is fundamentally constrained by the benchmarks they optimize against. Existing benchmarks are poorly aligned with production inference frameworks: they...

item.title: EvalVerse: Pipeline-Aware and Expert-Calibrated Benchmarking for Professional Cinematic Video Generation
item.description: The rapid evolution of generative video foundation models has propelled the field toward professional-grade cinematic synthesis. To achieve such demanding quality, the community transitions towards Reinforcement Learning (RL) and agentic...

item.title: D^2-Monitor: Dynamic Safety Monitoring for Diffusion LLMs via Hesitation-Aware Routing
item.description: Despite the emergence of diffusion large language models (D-LLMs) as an alternative to autoregressive large language models (AR-LLMs), safety monitoring for D-LLMs remains largely unexplored. Unlike AR-LLMs, D-LLMs generate text through...

item.title: QUACK: Questioning, Understanding, and Auditing Communicated Knowledge in Multimodal Social Deduction Agents
item.description: Social deduction games have become a popular testbed for probing reasoning, deception, coordination, and belief modeling in Large Language Model (LLM) agents. However, most environments are scored only by game outcomes such as win rates...

item.title: Beyond Final Answers: Auditing Trajectory-Level Hallucinations in Multi-Agent Industrial Workflows
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents that reason, use tools, and act over multiple steps. Yet most hallucination benchmarks still evaluate only the final output, missing failures that originate in i...

item.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
item.description: Vision-language models (VLMs) commonly formulate visual grounding and detection as a coordinate-token generation problem, serializing each 2D box into multiple 1D tokens that are learned and decoded largely independently. This token-by-t...

item.title: LongAV-Compass: Towards Unified Evaluation of Minute-Scale Audio-Visual Generation Across T2AV, I2AV, and V2AV
item.description: Audio-visual generation is rapidly advancing from short clips to minute-long content, while existing evaluation protocols remain largely confined to short-form settings. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on 5--10 second text-conditione...

item.title: DarkForest: Less Talk, Higher Accuracy for Multi-Agent LLMs
item.description: Multi-agent LLM systems improve reasoning by combining outputs from multiple agents, but interaction-heavy methods can introduce error propagation and high communication overhead. When agents exchange raw responses or reasoning traces, i...

item.title: RT-Lynx: Putting the GEMM Sparsity In a Right Way for Diffusion Models
item.description: Diffusion Transformers (DiT) achieve strong performance in image generation but incur substantial inference costs. While prior work has reduced this cost via quantization and distillation, semi-structured sparsity, which can nearly halve...

item.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
item.description: Subject-driven image generation aims to synthesize new images that preserve the identity of the given subject while following textual instructions. Existing approaches often encode text and reference images separately. This limits cross-...

item.title: MRT: Masked Region Transformer for Layered Image Generation and Editing at Scale
item.description: Layered image generation and editing is a fundamental capability that enables layer-wise reuse, editing, and composition of generated visual content, analogous to word-level editing in natural language. Despite its importance, this remai...

item.title: Agentic CLEAR: Automating Multi-Level Evaluation of LLM Agents
item.description: Agentic systems are becoming more capable: agents define strategies, take actions, and interact with different environments. This autonomy poses serious challenges for overseeing and assessing agent behavior. Most current tools are limit...

item.title: Geometry-Aware Representation Denoising for Robust Multi-view 3D Reconstruction
item.description: Multi-view 3D reconstruction has achieved remarkable progress with the advent of feed-forward 3D reconstruction models. However, these models are typically trained and evaluated under ideal, degradation-free imaging conditions, whereas r...

item.title: The MiniMax-M2 Series: Mini Activations Unleashing Max Real-World Intelligence
item.description: We introduce the MiniMax-M2 series, a family of Mixture-of-Experts language models built around the principle that mini activations can unleash maximum real-world intelligence. The flagship M2 contains 229.9B total parameters with only 9...

item.title: Share More, Search Less: Collaborative Parallel Thinking for Efficient Test-Time Scaling
item.description: Test-Time Scaling (TTS) enhances the reasoning capabilities of large language models by allocating additional inference compute to explore the solution space. However, existing parallel TTS methods typically keep branches isolated during...

item.title: LLaVA-OneVision-2: Towards Next-Generation Perceptual Intelligence
item.description: We introduce LLaVA-OneVision-2 (LLaVA-OV-2), the most capable vision-language model in the LLaVA-OneVision series to date, achieving superior performance across a broad range of multimodal benchmarks. The model builds on a native OneVisi...

item.title: Gemini Embedding 2: A Native Multimodal Embedding Model from Gemini
item.description: We introduce Gemini Embedding 2, a native multimodal embedding model that allows embedding video, audio, image, and text modalities in a unified representation space. We leverage the multimodal capabilities of Gemini to produce embedding...

item.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
item.description: Large language model (LLM) agents rely on reusable skills to solve complex tasks. However, existing skill creation approaches treat skills as isolated and static artifacts, limiting their reusability, reliability, and long-term improveme...

item.title: Efficient Agentic Reinforcement Learning with On-Policy Intrinsic Knowledge Boundary Enhancement
item.description: Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) has proven effective for training LLM-based agents with external tool-use capabilities. However, we identify that agentic RL training induces increasing redundant tool calls and blurs the model's intri...

item.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) are typically trained on shuffled corpora, yielding models whose knowledge is frozen at train time and whose temporal grounding remains poorly understood. In this work, we study the impact of pre-training dyn...

item.title: SpatialBench: Is Your Spatial Foundation Model an All-Round Player?
item.description: While spatial foundation models have demonstrated impressive performance on standard datasets, a critical question remains: are they truly all-round players capable of generalizing robustly across diverse downstream tasks, arbitrary view...

item.title: Soap2Soap: Long Cinematic Video Remaking via Multi-Agent Collaboration
item.description: We study series-level cinematic remaking, a long-horizon video-to-video generation problem that localizes full episodes or films via stylization or actor replacement while strictly preserving narrative structure, motion choreography, and...

item.title: Negligible in Size, Significant in Effect: On Scale Vectors in Large Language Models
item.description: Normalization layers in modern large language models (LLMs) consist of a deterministic normalization operation and a learnable scale vector. While the normalization operation has been extensively studied, the scale vector remains poorly...

item.title: SAM: State-Adaptive Memory for Long-Horizon Reasoning Agent
item.description: Long-horizon agentic reasoning requires large language models to act over long interaction histories containing thoughts, tool calls, observations, and partial conclusions. The challenge is not merely that these histories grow long, but...

item.title: ZeroUnlearn: Few-Shot Knowledge Unlearning in Large Language Models
item.description: Large language models inevitably retain sensitive information, defined as inputs that may induce harmful generations, due to training on massive web corpora, raising concerns for privacy and safety. Existing machine unlearning methods pr...

item.title: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check
item.description: Can large language models detect and report their own internal states? A number of studies have argued that the answer to this question is yes. We argue, based on lessons from human metacognition research, that this conclusion may be pre...

item.title: CroCo: Cross-Lingual Contrastive Preference Tuning on Self-Generations
item.description: Prior work establishes that controlled contrastiveness between self-generated responses from large language models, set via reward scores, improves downstream preference tuning in English. We extend this method to multiple languages and...

item.title: STREAM: A Data-Centric Framework for Mining High-Value Task-Oriented Dialogues from Streaming Media
item.description: Large language models for vertical domains are bottlenecked by the scarcity of complex, domain-specific task-oriented dialogues. Existing data acquisition pipelines face a persistent trilemma: expert annotation is expensive, real-world s...

item.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
item.description: We present MobileGym, a browser-hosted, lightweight, fully controllable environment for everyday mobile use, targeting interaction fidelity without replicating proprietary backends. It enables two capabilities previously out of reach for...

item.title: JLT: Clean-Latent Prediction in Latent Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Flow matching with clean-data prediction has shown that regressing the clean point can exploit low-dimensional structure more effectively than predicting an ambient noised quantity. We ask whether this principle remains useful after imag...

item.title: Balancing Fidelity and Diversity in Diffusion Models via Symmetric Attention Decomposition: Hopfield Perspective
item.description: We characterize the pre-softmax attention matrix QK^top in transformers as an associative memory matrix encoding pairwise associations between input features. By decomposing this matrix into its symmetric and skew-symmetric parts, we int...

item.title: Rethinking VLM Representation for VLA Initialization
item.description: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models widely adopt pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as policy backbones, yet it remains unclear what kind of pretrained VLM representation is useful as a VLA initialization. In this paper, we study V...

item.title: Confidence and Calibration of Activation Oracles for Reliable Interpretation of Language Model Internals
item.description: Activation oracles aim to make the activations of other models legible to humans and yield promising results compared to white-box interpretability techniques. However, uncertainty quantification (UQ) for the natural-language outputs of...

item.title: NSF-SciFy: Mining the NSF Awards Database for Scientific Claims
item.description: We introduce NSF-SciFy, a comprehensive dataset of scientific claims and investigation proposals extracted from National Science Foundation award abstracts. While previous scientific claim verification datasets have been limited in size...

item.title: EverAnimate: Minute-Scale Human Animation via Latent Flow Restoration
item.description: We propose EverAnimate, an efficient post-training method for long-horizon animated video generation that preserves visual quality and character identity. Long-form animation remains challenging because highly dynamic human motion must b...

item.title: Does Seeing More Mean Knowing More? Mono-Anchored Advantage Normalization for Multi-Source Visual Reasoning
item.description: Visual reasoning through reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable progress. However, when dealing with multi-source inputs, existing approaches tend to treat them as a mere accumulation of information...

item.title: Learning High-Frequency Continuous Action Chunks in Latent Space
item.description: Modern robotic policies increasingly rely on action chunking to execute complex tasks in the physical world. While action chunking improves temporal consistency at moderate action frequencies, it becomes insufficient when the action freq...
